# Global Rules

## Communication
- Always respond in English
- Be concise and direct

## Code Style
- Use TypeScript strict mode
- Prefer functional patterns
- Write JSDoc for public APIs

## Git Workflow
- Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, docs:, refactor:
- Never force-push to main
- Run tests before committing

## Paths
- Projects: /home/testuser/projects/

# Memory Index

- [user_role](memory/user_role.md): Senior fullstack developer, TypeScript + Rust
- [feedback_commits](memory/feedback_commits.md): Prefer squash merges for feature branches

---
name: code-reviewer
description: Reviews code for quality, security, and best practices. Use when you want a thorough code review before merging.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep
---

You are a senior code reviewer. Focus on:
- Security vulnerabilities (OWASP Top 10)
- Performance bottlenecks
- Type safety and null handling
- Test coverage gaps

Always provide actionable feedback with line references.

---
name: tester
description: Writes and runs tests. Follows red-green-refactor TDD cycle.
tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep
---

You are a test-first developer. For every feature:
1. Write failing tests first
2. Implement minimal code to pass
3. Refactor without breaking tests

Prefer integration tests over mocks where possible.

---
name: doc-writer
description: Generates and updates documentation. Use for README updates, API docs, and changelogs.
tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep
---

You write clear, accurate documentation. Keep docs close to code. Update changelogs with conventional commit format.

Review staged changes and write a conventional commit message. Run `git diff --staged`, summarize the changes, and create a commit with format: `type(scope): description`. Types: feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore.

Perform a thorough code review of the current branch diff against main. Check for security issues, logic errors, missing tests, and style violations. Output a structured report with severity levels: critical, warning, suggestion.
Prepare a release: bump version in package.json, update CHANGELOG.md with all commits since last tag, create a git tag, and output the release notes.

---
description: Deploy the current branch to staging environment
---

Run the deployment pipeline:
1. `npm run build` — verify build passes
2. `npm test` — verify all tests pass
3. `git push origin HEAD` — push to remote
4. Notify the team in Slack: deployment started

---
description: Initialize a new project with standard tooling
---

Set up a new TypeScript project:
1. Initialize package.json with `npm init -y`
2. Install TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier
3. Create tsconfig.json with strict mode
4. Add .eslintrc and .prettierrc
5. Create src/index.ts entry point
6. Add npm scripts: dev, build, test, lint

---
status: completed
title: Auth Middleware Refactor
date: 2026-03-20
---

# Auth Middleware Refactor

## Goal
Replace legacy session-based auth with JWT tokens to meet compliance requirements.

## Tasks
- [x] Audit existing auth middleware
- [x] Design JWT token structure
- [x] Implement token generation and validation
- [x] Update all protected routes
- [x] Write integration tests
- [x] Deploy to staging

## Notes
Legal flagged the old session storage mechanism. New implementation uses short-lived access tokens (15min) + refresh tokens (7 days).

---
status: in-progress
title: Backup Module Tests
date: 2026-03-24
---

# Backup Module Tests

## Goal
Add comprehensive test coverage for the backup/restore functionality.

## Tasks
- [x] Set up Vitest
- [x] Create Tauri API mocks
- [x] Create test fixtures
- [ ] Write unit tests
- [ ] Add edge case tests for large files
